assignment: Independently replay and audit the DKR-0 and DKR-1 candidate checkpoints. Decide each claim separately. Do not implement product changes or accept a claim whose active wall is breached, stale, unknown, self-certified, or incomplete.
human_clarification: Full activation of the statically declared dependency tree is intended.
Tests substitute through the scope seam instead of mocking the tree.
Runtime missing-tag failure is accepted in this frame.
Static tag availability analysis is deferred.
Hidden effect edges and implicit built-in tag injection remain forbidden.
required_checks: Run both saved replays from the feature worktree and retain exit status and parsed results.
Verify cited hashes or report exact mismatches.
Inspect enough cited source and tests to challenge judgement-heavy keep, reshape, and remove classifications.
Confirm whether current-owned session activation, nested reuse, sibling isolation, explicit checkpoint, and resume follow from the evidence.
Confirm or refute that ordinary activation cleanup calls beginFinish and changes runtime business status without an explicit finish.
Audit checkpoint completeness against the executable DKR checkpoint contract and frame v2 walls.
Explain whether a discovered baseline wall breach blocks only acceptance, blocks remediation, or requires a new reducing DKR under the skill contract. Do not invent an exception.
Run OKRA store verification after recording output.
forbidden: Product code, tests, docs, manifests, git history, PR state, external services, frame, tree, or another worker's artifacts
Self-certification, silent wall weakening, or treating the human clarification as permission for hidden effects
required_outputs: workers/validator-dkr-0-1/progress.jsonl via the absolute OKRA store helper worker-report command
terminal_condition: Finish after both candidates have explicit verified, rejected, or blocked dispositions with replay evidence and a governed progress record.
